drills.title: Infield Throwing Chain
drills.description: Players at SS and 2B practice quick catch-and-throw footwork, making throws to 1B in a continuous rotation.

drills.title: Around the Horn Infield Throws
drills.description: Full infield works on crisp throws around the horn (home-3rd-2nd-1st-home) focusing on accuracy and communication.

drills.title: Rapid Fire to First Base
drills.description: Coach rolls grounders to SS and 3B who make quick, accurate throws to 1B, emphasizing proper grip and set-up.

drills.title: Double-Play Feeds for 8–10s
drills.description: Middle infielders work on short, accurate feeds to turn double plays, using soft tosses and step-behind footwork.

drills.title: Bucket to First Base Drill
drills.description: Players rotate through SS, 2B, and 3B, fielding rolled balls and throwing into a bucket at 1B for accuracy.

drills.title: Relay Race Throwing Warm-Up
drills.description: Younger players practice basic throwing mechanics in relay teams, focusing on stepping toward the target.

drills.title: Tee Work Hitting Basics
drills.description: Players hit off a batting tee to learn stance, grip, and swing path with simple target goals.

drills.title: Outfield Fly Ball Call-Off
drills.description: Outfielders practice communicating and taking charge on fly balls to avoid collisions.

drills.title: Ladder Footwork for Infielders
drills.description: Advanced infielders use an agility ladder to develop quick, balanced feet before fielding.

drills.title: Bullpen Command Session
drills.description: Pitchers throw structured bullpen sets working on fastball command and simple off-speed pitches.

drills.title: Live BP Rotation Stations
drills.description: Hitters rotate through live BP, soft toss, and tee stations to reinforce consistent swings.

drills.title: Catcher Blocking Basics
drills.description: Catchers practice dropping to their knees and keeping balls in front on short hops and pitches in the dirt.

drills.title: Primary Lead-Off & Steal Breaks
drills.description: Runners practice proper leads and first-step explosiveness for stealing bases.

drills.title: Team Defense Communication Game
drills.description: Entire defense responds to simulated plays, calling out responsibilities before the pitch.

drills.title: Long Toss Progression
drills.description: Older players work through structured long toss to build arm strength safely.

drills.title: Short Hop Infield Challenge
drills.description: Advanced infielders field a series of short hops and tough hops to improve glove skills.

policies.title: Rainout & Weather Policy
policies.content:
Our league prioritizes player safety when making weather-related decisions. Decisions consider field conditions, visibility, lightning, and temperature.

### Same-Day Game Cancellations
Most same-day cancellations are decided 60–90 minutes before the scheduled start time whenever possible. For early games, decisions may be made earlier in the day after field inspections.

If severe weather develops suddenly, games may be delayed, suspended, or canceled at the field by the on-site league official or umpire crew. Coaches will be notified via text and email, and updates will be posted on the homepage and schedule page.

Parents and guardians should always use their own judgment regarding travel safety. If you feel conditions are unsafe to travel, notify your coach as soon as possible.

### Lightning & Severe Storms
At the first sign of lightning or audible thunder, play will be suspended immediately and all participants must clear the field and metal bleachers. A minimum 30-minute wait is required after the last observed lightning or thunder before resuming.

### Make-Up Games
The league will make reasonable efforts to reschedule rainouts, prioritizing division games that impact standings. Make-ups may be scheduled on weeknights or weekend afternoons depending on field availability.

policies.title: Player, Coach & Spectator Code of Conduct
policies.content: All participants are expected to demonstrate sportsmanship, respect, and integrity. Harassment of players, coaches, umpires, or other spectators will not be tolerated. Coaches are responsible for modeling positive behavior and ensuring that all players receive encouragement and fair playing time appropriate to the division.

policies.title: Player Safety Guidelines
policies.content: Helmets must be worn when batting, on deck, and running the bases. Catchers must wear full protective gear during games and while warming up pitchers. Bats should never be swung outside of designated batting areas. Any player suspected of injury, including concussion symptoms, must be removed from play and evaluated by a qualified adult.

policies.title: Registration & Refund Policy
policies.content: Registrations are accepted on a first-come, first-served basis until divisions reach capacity. Full payment or an active payment plan is required to secure a roster spot. Refunds requested before uniforms are ordered may be granted minus a processing fee. After uniforms are ordered, refunds are limited to documented medical situations or league cancellations.

clinics.name: Saturday Power Hitting Clinic (Ages 11–12)
clinics.description: Small-group batting clinic focusing on swing path, contact point, and using the lower half. Includes tee work, front toss, and short cage rounds.

clinics.name: Saturday Line-Drive Batting Clinic (Ages 11–12)
clinics.description: Emphasis on line-drive contact and situational hitting. Players rotate through tee, soft toss, and front toss stations.

clinics.name: Saturday Advanced Batting Clinic (Ages 11–12)
clinics.description: Higher-intensity batting session with live pitching, timing drills, and situational hitting games.

clinics.name: Wednesday Evening Hitting Series (Ages 11–12)
clinics.description: Four-week evening series building consistent mechanics through tee work, cage reps, and video review.

clinics.name: Saturday Skills Camp (Ages 8–10)
clinics.description: Introductory camp covering throwing, fielding, basic hitting, and baserunning in a fun game-based format.

clinics.name: Friday Pitching Foundations (Ages 11–12)
clinics.description: Pitching clinic covering grip, balance point, and safe mechanics for developing pitchers.

clinics.name: Sunday Catching Clinic (Ages 11–12)
clinics.description: Blocking, receiving, and throw-down mechanics for catchers preparing for Minors and Majors.

clinics.name: Infield Fundamentals (Ages 8–10)
clinics.description: Footwork, ready position, and basic infield throwing progressions for developing infielders.

clinics.name: Speed & Baserunning Clinic (Ages 11–12)
clinics.description: Lead-offs, turns, and aggressive but smart baserunning techniques.

clinics.name: Rookie Skills Day (Ages 5–7)
clinics.description: Fun introduction to baseball for new players with parent participation encouraged.

clinics.name: Summer Power Hitting Camp (Ages 13–15)
clinics.description: Advanced hitters work on exit velocity, launch angle awareness, and situational power.

clinics.name: Defense & All-Skills Clinic (Ages 11–12)
clinics.description: Well-rounded clinic combining infield, outfield, and throwing mechanics.
